# Daily Report — 2026-04-27

## Task Completed
Added CMDB relationship tools: `list_ci_relationships`, `get_ci_relationship`,
`create_ci_relationship`, `delete_ci_relationship`, and `list_ci_relationship_types`.

The new `cmdb_relationship_tools.py` module manages records in the `cmdb_rel_ci` junction
table and queries available relationship types from `cmdb_rel_type`. Key features:

- `list_ci_relationships` — paginated list with filters for parent CI, child CI, and
  relationship type sys_id; also accepts raw encoded query strings.
- `get_ci_relationship` — fetch a single cmdb_rel_ci record by sys_id.
- `create_ci_relationship` — POST a new directional relationship given parent, child, and
  relationship type sys_ids.
- `delete_ci_relationship` — DELETE a cmdb_rel_ci record; handles both 204 and 404.
- `list_ci_relationship_types` — paginated list of cmdb_rel_type records with optional
  name substring filter; surfaces parent_descriptor / child_descriptor labels.

Reference values returned by ServiceNow (dict `{value, display_value}`) are transparently
normalised to plain strings by `_format_relationship`. Tools registered in `__init__.py`,
`tool_utils.py`, `tool_packages.yaml` (`system_administrator` and `full` packages).

## Test Results
- Total tests: 767 passed, 4 deselected
- Coverage: 90% overall; cmdb_relationship_tools.py at 98%
- All passing: yes

## Security
System-level vulnerabilities in cryptography, mcp, pip, pyjwt, setuptools, wheel — not
in project code.

## Next Up
Add asset management tools (alm_asset table): list_assets, get_asset, update_asset for
tracking hardware and software assets linked to CMDB CIs.
